title: UN Food Systems Youth Leadership Programme 2026–2028: Global Leadership Opportunity for Young Changemakers Transforming Food Systems Worldwide
summary: Applications are now open for the second cohort of the United Nations Food Systems Youth Leadership Programme (YLP), a prestigious global initiative designed to empower young leaders who are committed to transforming food systems and advancing sustainable development around the world. Led by the United Nations Food Systems Coordination Hub and supported by the German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ), the Youth Leadership Programme brings together emerging leaders from diverse backgrounds to strengthen their leadership capacity, expand their networks, and accelerate meaningful action toward sustainable, inclusive, and resilient food systems. Building on the success of its inaugural cohort, the programme is now expanding its global network of changemakers working across policy, entrepreneurship, science, advocacy, research, agriculture, climate action, and community development. Participants will gain access to a transformative leadership journey that equips them with practical skills, mentorship, peer learning opportunities, and exposure to global food systems processes. For young leaders seeking to influence food systems transformation at local, national, regional, and global levels, this programme offers an exceptional opportunity to join a growing international community dedicated to creating lasting impact. About the UN Food Systems Youth Leadership Programme The UN Food Systems Youth Leadership Programme is more than a conventional training programme. It is a long-term leadership development initiative designed to nurture the next generation of leaders working at the intersection of food systems, sustainability, climate resilience, economic development, and social inclusion. The programme recognizes that young people play a critical role in addressing some of the world s most pressing challenges, including: Food insecurity Climate change Sustainable agriculture Nutrition and health Environmental sustainability Rural development Social equity Economic inclusion Through structured learning, mentorship, collaboration, and leadership development, participants gain the tools and knowledge necessary to create meaningful change within their communities, institutions, organizations, and countries. Why Food Systems Transformation Matters Food systems touch every aspect of human life. They influence: Food production Nutrition Public health Climate resilience Biodiversity Employment Economic development Social inclusion However, food systems around the world face growing pressures, including: Climate-related disruptions Population growth Resource scarcity Economic inequalities Environmental degradation Food insecurity Addressing these challenges requires innovative leadership, cross-sector collaboration, and systems thinking. The Youth Leadership Programme aims to equip young leaders with the skills and perspectives needed to address these complex issues effectively. Building on the Success of the First Cohort The first cohort of the Youth Leadership Programme demonstrated the power of youth-led action and collaboration. Over the past two years, participants have: Strengthened food systems initiatives in their countries Influenced policy discussions Advanced research and innovation Led advocacy campaigns Built partnerships across sectors Created collaborations that extend beyond borders The impact of the programme continues to grow through the work of its alumni network. One notable participant, Sofia Bacquerizo from Ecuador, highlighted the transformative nature of the programme by explaining how the relationships and knowledge gained through the YLP contributed directly to legislative reforms she is advancing in her country s National Assembly. Her experience illustrates how the programme helps participants turn ideas into tangible action and policy influence. What Participants Will Gain The Youth Leadership Programme offers a wide range of benefits designed to strengthen participants leadership capabilities and increase their impact. 1. Systems Thinking and Leadership Skills Participants will develop a deeper understanding of food systems and learn how to navigate complex challenges through systems thinking approaches. Key areas include: Strategic leadership Problem-solving Stakeholder engagement Collaborative decision-making Systems analysis 2. Access to a Global Network Participants will become part of an international community of: Youth leaders Policymakers Researchers Entrepreneurs Development practitioners UN experts Food systems specialists This network creates opportunities for long-term collaboration and knowledge exchange. 3. Collaboration and Peer Learning The programme emphasizes learning through collaboration. Participants will engage with peers from different countries, cultures, and professional backgrounds, enabling them to: Share experiences Exchange ideas Explore innovative solutions Develop joint initiatives 4. Practical Leadership Tools Participants will gain practical resources and methodologies that help strengthen their leadership effectiveness and project impact. These tools can be applied directly to: Community initiatives Policy advocacy Research projects Entrepreneurship ventures Development programmes 5. Exposure to Global Food Systems Processes The programme provides valuable insight into international food systems governance and development processes. Participants will gain exposure to: United Nations initiatives Global food systems dialogues International partnerships Policy frameworks Sustainable development agendas A Leadership Journey, Not Just a Training Programme One of the most distinctive aspects of the Youth Leadership Programme is its long-term approach to leadership development. Rather than focusing solely on technical training, the programme helps participants: Build confidence Expand influence Strengthen leadership capacity Develop strategic thinking skills Foster meaningful relationships This holistic approach supports participants in becoming effective leaders capable of driving change at multiple levels. Who Should Apply? The programme seeks young leaders who are actively contributing to food systems transformation and sustainable development. Applications are particularly encouraged from: Young Farmers Individuals working to improve agricultural systems and food production practices. Entrepreneurs Founders and innovators creating solutions for food systems challenges. Policy Advocates Young professionals engaged in policy development, advocacy, and governance. Researchers Individuals conducting research related to food systems, sustainability, climate, agriculture, nutrition, or development. Science Communicators Professionals who translate scientific knowledge into accessible information for broader audiences. Indigenous and Local Community Representatives Leaders working to preserve and promote community knowledge and sustainable practices. Civil Society Leaders Individuals leading initiatives that contribute to social, environmental, and economic development. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet all of the following requirements: Age Requirement Applicants must be between 18 and 35 years old. Language Requirement Applicants must be proficient in English. Nationality Requirement Applicants must be nationals of low- or middle-income countries. Priority regions include: Africa Asia and the Pacific Latin America and the Caribbean Professional Experience Requirement Applicants should demonstrate experience in: Food systems Sustainable development Climate action Related fields Leadership Experience Applicants must have experience leading or contributing to initiatives within: Communities Organizations Institutions Sectors Programme Commitment Applicants must be willing to participate fully in programme activities through 2028. Why You Should Apply The Youth Leadership Programme is ideal for young changemakers seeking to: Strengthen leadership skills Expand professional networks Increase policy influence Collaborate internationally Advance food systems transformation Contribute to sustainable development goals Build long-term partnerships Access mentorship opportunities The programme provides a unique platform to amplify youth voices in global conversations and decision-making processes. Important Deadline Applications Close: 13 July 2026 Interested candidates are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline to maximize their chances of consideration. APPLY HERE How This Programme Can Shape Your Future Participation in the Youth Leadership Programme can open doors to: International collaborations Leadership opportunities Policy engagement Professional growth Research partnerships Advocacy initiatives Global development networks Participants join a growing community of leaders working across sectors to address complex food systems challenges and create sustainable solutions. Click HERE to learn more about UN Food Systems Youth Leadership Programme 2026–2028. Conclusion The UN Food Systems Youth Leadership Programme 2026–2028 represents an outstanding opportunity for young leaders from low- and middle-income countries to develop the skills, networks, and knowledge needed to drive transformative change in food systems and sustainable development. Through mentorship, systems thinking, peer learning, and global collaboration, participants will gain valuable experience that strengthens their ability to influence policies, lead initiatives, and create lasting impact within their communities and beyond. For young farmers, entrepreneurs, researchers, advocates, climate leaders, and development practitioners who are passionate about creating more sustainable, equitable, and resilient food systems, this programme offers an unparalleled platform for leadership growth and global engagement. Interested candidates should submit their applications before 13 July 2026 and take the next step toward becoming part of a powerful international network of changemakers. Discover more global Fellowship , Leadership Development , and UN opportunities on Opportunities for Youths (OFY). title: Unlock the Power of Intellectual Property: Your Complete Guide to 16 Free WIPO Online Courses (2026–2027)
summary: In today‘s innovation-driven global economy, intellectual property has become one of the most valuable assets any individual, business, or nation can possess. Whether you are an entrepreneur protecting a groundbreaking invention, a creative professional safeguarding your artistic works, a legal practitioner advising clients on IP strategy, or a policymaker shaping the innovation landscape, understanding intellectual property is no longer optional—it is essential. The World Intellectual Property Organization, through its WIPO Academy, offers a comprehensive suite of free online courses designed to take learners from absolute beginner to advanced specialist. With 16 courses covering everything from copyright and patents to trademarks, traditional knowledge, arbitration, software licensing, and export strategy, this is one of the most valuable free educational resources available anywhere in the world. This guide provides a complete overview of every course in the WIPO Academy portfolio available on the UN SDG:Learn platform. Whether you are just starting your IP journey or looking to deepen your expertise in a specific area, you will find detailed information about each course, including level, duration, language availability, registration deadlines, and learning objectives. All courses are offered entirely free of charge by the World Intellectual Property Organization, a United Nations partner agency, and are available in multiple languages including English, French, Spanish, Arabic, Russian, and Portuguese. Course Portfolio Overview The WIPO Academy course体系 follows a progressive learning pathway. Beginners start with foundational courses that build core knowledge, then advance to specialized courses tailored to specific professional interests and career goals. The two introductory courses—DL-001 and DL-101—are strongly recommended as prerequisites for all advanced courses. PART ONE: FOUNDATIONAL COURSES DL-001: Primer on Intellectual Property Course Overview: This self-paced introductory course provides a comprehensive overview of the fundamental aspects of intellectual property, covering copyright and related rights, trademarks, patents, geographical indications, and industrial designs. It also explores current topics impacting IP, including plant variety rights, unfair competition, the enforcement of IP rights, and emerging issues in the field. Course Structure: The course consists of 12 sequential modules designed to help participants progressively build their understanding of IP concepts. Self-assessment tools are woven throughout the modules to help learners test their knowledge and ability to apply concepts and key facts. Assessment and Certification: There is no final examination. Participants move through the modules based on self-assessed progress and readiness. Upon completion, learners are encouraged to move on to DL-101. Key Details: Level: Introductory Duration: 3 hours Effort: 3 hours Languages: English, French, Russian, Spanish, Arabic, Portuguese Price: Free Certificate: No Start Date: 30 January 2027 End Date: 15 January 2027 Registration Deadline: 12 October 2027 Target Audience: This course is designed for anyone seeking a general overview of IP fundamentals—no prior expertise in the field is required. How to Access: Click Visit course page at the top of the course page. Create a WIPO Academy account, set up your profile, and register on the WIPO Academy platform. DL-101: General Course on Intellectual Property Course Overview: Building on the concepts introduced in DL-001, this course provides a more comprehensive presentation of the main areas of intellectual property. It covers copyright, related rights, patents, trademarks, geographical indications, industrial designs, plant variety protection, unfair competition, and the treaties administered by WIPO. Course Structure: The General Course on Intellectual Property consists of 13 self-paced modules. Self-assessment tools are strategically placed at the end of each module to help participants gauge their understanding, monitor their progress, and evaluate their ability to apply the concepts and information covered. Assessment and Certification: A time-bound final examination, delivered online and composed of multiple-choice questions, awards successful participants an electronic certificate of completion. Key Details: Level: Introductory Duration: 6 weeks Effort: 55 hours Languages: English, French Price: Free Certificate: Yes Start Date: 30 January 2026 End Date: 15 December 2026 Registration Deadline: 10 December 2026 Target Audience: This course is designed for beginners, students, and professionals seeking a solid, self-guided introduction to the IP system. It is suitable for: Students, Researchers, and Academics Entrepreneurs, Startup Founders, and Innovators Legal Professionals and Paralegals Government Officials and Policymakers Business Professionals and Managers Artists, Designers, and Creators General Public and Lifelong Learners Learning Objectives: By the end of the course, participants will be able to: Understand the fundamental concepts of intellectual property Recognize the role of IP in society and the economy Identify the main types of IP rights and their functions Describe the purpose, scope, and duration of key IP rights Understand the international framework for IP protection Analyze the application of IP in different contexts Interpret the relationship between IP and development Apply basic IP knowledge practically How to Access: Click Visit course page at the top of the course page. Create a WIPO Academy account, set up your profile, and register on the WIPO Academy platform. PART TWO: ADVANCED SPECIALIZED COURSES The following advanced courses are designed for learners who have completed DL-101 and wish to deepen their expertise in specific areas of intellectual property. All advanced courses are free, self-paced, and do not offer certificates unless otherwise noted. DL-201: Copyright and Related Rights Course Overview: This advanced course covers the fundamental principles of international copyright and related rights protection, providing a comprehensive overview of international treaties, recent developments, and trends in the area of international copyright. It describes the system of collective management of rights and addresses issues of copyright and related rights in the digital environment. The course also covers the role of WIPO in the worldwide protection of copyright. Prerequisites: Completion of DL-101 is strongly recommended. Course Structure: The course is composed of 13 modules, each containing specific learning objectives. Key Details: Level: Advanced Effort: 100 hours total Languages: English, French Price: Free Certificate: No Start Date: 30 January 2026 End Date: 12 March 2027 Registration Deadline: 12 October 2026 Target Audience: This course is designed for individuals seeking an advanced understanding of international copyright and related rights, including: Legal professionals Government officials and policymakers Academics, researchers, and advanced students Staff of copyright offices and collective management organizations Professionals in the creative industries WIPO personnel and IP office staff Note: There is no tutorial service, exam, or certificate offered for this course. DL-203: Intellectual Property, Traditional Knowledge and Traditional Cultural Expressions Course Overview: This self-paced advanced course introduces key concepts and policy issues in the protection of traditional knowledge and traditional cultural expressions through intellectual property. It provides technical information on key concepts, international, regional, and national experiences, policy options, and legal mechanisms available or under consideration for the IP protection of traditional knowledge and traditional cultural expressions. Prerequisites: Completion of DL-101 is strongly recommended. Key Details: Level: Advanced Effort: 100 hours total Languages: English, French, Russian, Spanish Price: Free Certificate: No Start Date: 30 January 2026 End Date: 12 March 2027 Registration Deadline: 12 October 2026 Target Audience: This course is particularly suited for: Legal professionals Government officials and policymakers Academics, researchers, and advanced students Staff of copyright offices and collective management organizations Professionals in the creative industries WIPO personnel and IP office staff Learning Objectives: Learners will examine legal mechanisms currently available or under discussion, including those under negotiation at WIPO. Note: There is no tutorial service, exam, or certificate offered for this course. DL-301: Patents Course Overview: DL-301 provides participants with an in-depth perspective on the principles and economic impact of patents and the patenting process as a whole. The course examines the traditional use of patents by centers of knowledge such as universities and other specialized institutions of higher learning. It also provides a comprehensive review of the importance of patent pools in facilitating the effective transfer and development of technology. Prerequisites: Completion of DL-101 is highly recommended. Key Details: Level: Advanced Length: 100 hours Effort: Self-paced Languages: English, French, Russian, Spanish Price: Free Certificate: No Start Date: 30 January 2026 End Date: 15 December 2026 Registration Deadline: 12 October 2026 Target Audience: DL-301 is designed for individuals seeking an advanced understanding of the patent system, innovation management, technology transfer, or related fields, including: Legal professionals Government officials and policymakers Academics, researchers, and advanced students Staff of copyright offices and collective management organizations Professionals in the creative industries WIPO personnel and IP office staff Learning Objectives: Participants will explore the legal framework of patents, the requirements for patentability, and the strategic role patents play in innovation and economic development. Note: There are no tutorials or exams for this course, and no certificate is offered. DL-302: Trademarks, Industrial Designs and Geographical Indications Course Overview: DL-302 covers intellectual property aspects closely related to trade and competition in the globalized market. It educates and raises awareness of complex issues surrounding the protection and management of intellectual property for branding. The course serves as a preparatory course designed to precede practical training in the protection of trademarks, industrial designs, and geographical indications. Prerequisites: Completion of DL-101 is recommended. Key Details: Level: Advanced Languages: English, French, Russian, Spanish Price: Free Certificate: No Start Date: 30 January 2026 End Date: 15 December 2026 Registration Deadline: 12 October 2026 Target Audience: This course is designed for: Legal professionals Government officials and policymakers Academics, researchers, and advanced students Staff of copyright offices and collective management organizations Professionals in the creative industries WIPO personnel and IP office staff Learning Objectives: Participants will be able to: Explain the legal frameworks and key concepts governing the protection of trademarks, industrial designs, and geographical indications Compare and contrast different national and regional approaches to trademarks, designs, and geographical indications Apply advanced knowledge to case studies involving complex IP disputes or policy challenges Note: There is no tutorial service, exam, or certificate offered for this course. DL-317: Arbitration and Mediation Procedure under the WIPO Rules Course Overview: This advanced online course provides a comprehensive overview of the principal features and advantages of the procedures administered by the WIPO Arbitration and Mediation Center. It describes the main features and advantages of the procedures designed by the Center, the selection process of the most appropriate dispute resolution procedure for any given case, and how to draft appropriate dispute resolution clauses for insertion into an agreement. Prerequisites: Completion of DL-101 is strongly recommended. Key Details: Level: Advanced Length: 100 hours total Effort: Self-paced Languages: English, French, Russian, Spanish Price: Free Certificate: No Start Date: 1 June 2028 End Date: 12 March 2027 Registration Deadline: 12 October 2026 Target Audience: This course is particularly suited for: Legal professionals Government officials and policymakers Academics, researchers, and advanced students Staff of copyright offices and collective management organizations Professionals in the creative industries WIPO personnel and IP office staff Learning Objectives: Participants will: Understand the main features and advantages of the procedures designed by the WIPO Arbitration and Mediation Center Analyze different case scenarios to evaluate and select the most appropriate dispute resolution procedure Apply drafting principles to create appropriate dispute resolution clauses for inclusion in agreements Note: There is no tutorial service, exam, or certificate offered for this course. DL-318: Patent Information Search Course Overview: This course is designed to introduce and illustrate key concepts in patent searching. Practical activities have been incorporated throughout the course to complement theoretical principles that underpin patent information searching. Participants are furnished with in-depth information regarding sources, techniques, and data used during patent searches. Prerequisites: Completion of DL-101 is strongly recommended. Key Details: Level: Advanced Length: 100 hours Effort: Self-paced Languages: English, French, Spanish Price: Free Certificate: No Start Date: 30 January 2026 End Date: 15 December 2026 Registration Deadline: 10 December 2026 Target Audience: The course is particularly suited for: Legal professionals Government officials and policymakers Academics, researchers, and advanced students Staff of copyright offices and collective management organizations Professionals in the creative industries WIPO personnel and IP office staff Learning Objectives: Participants will gain in-depth knowledge of the sources, techniques, and data used in conducting patent searches. Note: There is no tutorial service, exam, or certificate offered for this course. DL-320: Basics of Patent Drafting Course Overview: This advanced distance learning course introduces and illustrates the concepts of patent drafting. It is neither a qualification course for patent drafting nor a replacement of existing training manuals for patent attorneys or patent agents in countries with robust professional education systems. Some practical exercises are proposed with enough background and description to help learners gain confidence to form a credible set of claims. The course material has been designed on the basis of common denominators of several national patent laws in which most applicants seek patent protection. Prerequisites: Completion of DL-101 is strongly recommended. Key Details: Level: Advanced Length: Self-paced Effort: 100 hours Languages: English, French, Russian, Spanish, Arabic, Portuguese Price: Free Certificate: No Start Date: 30 January 2026 End Date: 15 December 2026 Registration Deadline: 10 December 2026 Target Audience: Legal professionals Government officials and policymakers Academics, researchers, and advanced students Staff of copyright offices and collective management organizations Professionals in the creative industries WIPO personnel and IP office staff Learning Objectives: Participants will be able to: Understand the fundamental concepts and principles of patent drafting Identify the key elements that make up a patent claim Apply drafting techniques through guided explanations and practical exercises Develop a structured and credible set of patent claims with increasing confidence Note: There is no tutorial service, exam, or certificate offered for this course. DL-450: Intellectual Property Management Course Overview: This course examines the economic and strategic dimensions of intellectual property. It focuses on IP from the perspective of why and how for participants who have already covered the basics of what IP is. The first three modules teach about the economic significance of IP, covering IP asset identification, IP incubation, IP commercialization, IP valuation, and IP taxation. The last three modules offer an in-depth look at commercial activities in the digital area, including e-commerce and IP, digital management of creative works, and the strategic management of IP. Prerequisites: Completion of DL-101 is strongly recommended. Key Details: Level: Advanced Length: Self-paced Effort: 100 hours Languages: English, French, Russian, Spanish, Arabic, Portuguese Price: Free Certificate: No Start Date: 30 January 2026 End Date: 15 December 2026 Registration Deadline: 10 December 2026 Target Audience: Legal professionals Government officials and policymakers Academics, researchers, and advanced students Staff of copyright offices and collective management organizations Professionals in the creative industries WIPO personnel and IP office staff Learning Objectives: Participants will be able to: Understand the economic significance of intellectual property and its role in business management Apply best practices for identifying, valuing, and commercializing IP assets Develop strategies for managing IP in digital environments and leveraging it for commercial advantage Note: There is no tutorial service, exam, or certificate offered for this course. PART THREE: COLLECTIVE MANAGEMENT OF COPYRIGHT AND RELATED RIGHTS – A Five-Course Series The following five courses examine collective management of copyright and related rights from the perspective of different stakeholders. Together, they form a comprehensive learning pathway for anyone seeking expertise in this specialized area. DL-501: Collective Management of Copyright and Related Rights for Legal Practitioners Course Overview: This course introduces legal practitioners to collective management of copyright, covering CMO operations, agreements, tariff disputes, and international treaties, while highlighting the benefits of collective management for creators, users, and society. Legal practitioners need to understand what CMOs do, how they acquire mandates from rights holders, and how they license the use of protected works. Prerequisites: Completion of DL-101 is strongly recommended. Key Details: Level: Advanced Length: Self-paced Effort: 100 hours Languages: English, French, Russian, Spanish, Arabic, Portuguese Price: Free Certificate: No Start Date: 30 January 2026 End Date: 15 December 2026 Registration Deadline: 10 December 2026 Target Audience: Legal professionals Government officials and policymakers Academics, researchers, and advanced students Staff of copyright offices and collective management organizations Professionals in the creative industries WIPO personnel and IP office staff Learning Objectives: Participants will understand the role and functions of collective management organizations and their legal framework, and apply knowledge of international copyright treaties to practical legal scenarios in collective management. Note: There is no tutorial service, exam, or certificate offered for this course. DL-502: Collective Management of Copyright and Related Rights for Rights Holders Course Overview: This course explores collective management of copyright, showing how CMOs operate across music, text, visual arts, and dramatic works, and highlighting benefits for creators, users, legislators, and society. It is targeted at anyone intending to learn in-depth about collective management of copyright, with a particular focus on primary rights holders. Prerequisites: Completion of DL-101 is strongly recommended. Key Details: Level: Advanced Length: Self-paced Effort: 100 hours Languages: English, French, Russian, Spanish, Arabic, Portuguese Price: Free Certificate: No Start Date: 30 January 2026 End Date: 15 December 2026 Registration Deadline: 10 December 2026 Target Audience: Legal professionals Government officials and policymakers Academics, researchers, and advanced students Staff of copyright offices and collective management organizations Professionals in the creative industries WIPO personnel and IP office staff Note: There is no tutorial service, exam, or certificate offered for this course. DL-503: Collective Management of Copyright and Related Rights for Collective Management Organizations (CMOs) Course Overview: This course is intended for anyone wishing to gain an in-depth understanding of collective management, in particular collective management organizations. It covers the workings of collective management of copyright in the categories of music, text and images, works of visual art and photography, and dramatic works. The course also discusses the day-to-day work of CMOs and their governance, and examines the challenges and opportunities brought about by digital technologies. Prerequisites: Completion of DL-101 is strongly recommended. Key Details: Level: Advanced Length: Self-paced Effort: 100 hours Languages: English, French, Russian, Spanish, Arabic, Portuguese Price: Free Certificate: No Start Date: 30 January 2026 End Date: 15 December 2026 Registration Deadline: 10 December 2026 Target Audience: Legal professionals Government officials and policymakers Academics, researchers, and advanced students Staff of copyright offices and collective management organizations Professionals in the creative industries WIPO personnel and IP office staff Learning Objectives: Participants will understand: The role and benefits of collective management for creators, users, legislators, and society How CMOs operate across different categories of copyright works The interaction of legislation, policy, and rights management in an effective copyright system The challenges and opportunities in collective management brought by digital technologies Note: There is no tutorial service, exam, or certificate offered for this course. DL-506: Collective Management of Copyright and Related Rights for Policy Makers Course Overview: This course introduces policy-making in copyright and collective management, exploring legislative frameworks, enforcement, and the role of government in supporting creators, users, and society across various creative sectors. Policy makers have a direct role in legislation and enforcement, with the primary role of laying down legislative frameworks for copyright and collective management. Prerequisites: Completion of DL-101 is strongly recommended. Key Details: Level: Advanced Length: Self-paced Effort: 100 hours Languages: English, French, Russian, Spanish, Arabic, Portuguese Price: Free Certificate: No Start Date: 30 January 2026 End Date: 15 December 2026 Registration Deadline: 10 December 2026 Target Audience: Legal professionals Government officials and policymakers Academics, researchers, and advanced students Staff of copyright offices and collective management organizations Professionals in the creative industries WIPO personnel and IP office staff Learning Objectives: Participants will: Understand the role of policy-makers in copyright legislation, enforcement, and collective management Compare different legislative frameworks for collective management and their impact on stakeholders Assess how government involvement in collective management benefits creators, users, and society Note: There is no tutorial service, exam, or certificate offered for this course. PART FOUR: OTHER ADVANCED SPECIALIZED COURSES DL-511: Software Licensing Including Open Source Course Overview: This course explores proprietary and open source software, their implications, and licensing. The objective is to enhance awareness of the possibilities and implications of proprietary and open source software, and to create useful sources of information including concrete examples and bibliography on software licensing, with special emphasis on the needs of developing countries. Prerequisites: Completion of DL-101 is strongly recommended. Key Details: Level: Advanced Length: Self-paced Effort: 100 hours Languages: English, French, Russian, Spanish, Arabic, Portuguese Price: Free Certificate: No Start Date: 30 January 2026 End Date: 15 December 2026 Registration Deadline: 10 December 2026 Target Audience: Legal professionals Government officials and policymakers Academics, researchers, and advanced students Staff of copyright offices and collective management organizations Professionals in the creative industries WIPO personnel and IP office staff Learning Objectives: Participants will: Understand the key differences and implications of proprietary and open source software Apply their learning to identify relevant licensing frameworks and their practical applications Create useful resources, including examples and bibliographies, to support software licensing decisions, with emphasis on developing country contexts Note: There is no tutorial service, exam, or certificate offered for this course. DL-730: Executive Course on Intellectual Property and Exports Course Overview: DL-730 explores how intellectual property supports the protection and commercialization of goods and services in exports, providing practical skills for exporters and export agency professionals. Intellectual property is a key tool that facilitates both the protection and commercialization of goods and services at national, regional, and international levels. The course uses a practical, skills-focused methodology to address the common challenges and issues faced by exporters and export agency workers in everyday practice. Prerequisites: Completion of DL-101 is strongly recommended. Key Details: Level: Advanced Length: Self-paced Effort: 100 hours Languages: English, Spanish, Portuguese Price: Free Certificate: No Start Date: 30 January 2026 End Date: 15 December 2026 Registration Deadline: 10 December 2026 Target Audience: Legal professionals Government officials and policymakers Academics, researchers, and advanced students Staff of copyright offices and collective management organizations Professionals in the creative industries WIPO personnel and IP office staff Learning Objectives: Participants will: Understand the role of intellectual property in the protection and commercialization of goods and services for export Learn to identify common IP-related challenges faced by exporters and export agencies Apply practical strategies to leverage IP effectively in international trade and market expansion Note: There is no tutorial service, exam, or certificate offered for this course. title: Harvard Radcliffe Institute Fellowship Program 2027–2028: Prestigious Fully Funded Fellowship at Harvard University for Scholars, Scientists, Writers, Journalists, Artists, and Public Intellectuals
summary: The Harvard Radcliffe Institute Fellowship Program 2027–2028 is now accepting applications from exceptional scholars, scientists, researchers, writers, journalists, artists, and public intellectuals from around the world. Hosted by the Harvard Radcliffe Institute for Advanced Study at Harvard University, this highly competitive fellowship offers participants a rare and transformative opportunity to spend a full academic year pursuing ambitious projects within one of the world s most intellectually vibrant academic environments. Widely recognized as one of the most prestigious interdisciplinary fellowship programs globally, the Harvard Radcliffe Institute Fellowship provides fellows with access to Harvard University s unparalleled resources, world-class faculty, renowned libraries, research facilities, and a dynamic community of thinkers and innovators. The fellowship is specifically designed for individuals who are pursuing innovative work that addresses important social, scientific, artistic, and policy challenges while engaging audiences beyond traditional academic circles. For those seeking time, resources, intellectual freedom, and interdisciplinary collaboration to advance a significant project, the Harvard Radcliffe Institute Fellowship Program represents an extraordinary opportunity. About the Harvard Radcliffe Institute Fellowship Program The Harvard Radcliffe Institute Fellowship Program brings together exceptional individuals from diverse disciplines and professional backgrounds to engage in advanced research, creative work, scholarly inquiry, and intellectual exploration. Unlike many traditional fellowships that focus on a single discipline or area of study, the Radcliffe Fellowship is intentionally interdisciplinary. It creates an environment where scientists collaborate with artists, historians exchange ideas with engineers, journalists interact with policy experts, and scholars engage with public intellectuals. This unique model encourages intellectual cross-pollination and helps generate innovative solutions to complex global challenges. Application Materials Applicants must submit a complete application package through the online application portal. Required materials include: 1. Online Application Form Applicants must complete all required sections. 2. Curriculum Vitae (CV) A current professional CV outlining achievements and experience. 3. Project Proposal Applicants must submit a project proposal of up to 1,400 words. The proposal should clearly explain: Research or creative objectives Methodology Expected outcomes Potential impact 4. title: GovAI Winter Fellowships 2027: Fully Funded AI Governance Research Fellowships in London and Washington, DC with £12,000 Salary or $21,000 Stipend
summary: Artificial intelligence is rapidly transforming governments, economies, industries, and societies around the world. As policymakers, researchers, and institutions grapple with the opportunities and challenges presented by advanced AI systems, the need for skilled professionals capable of shaping effective AI governance frameworks has never been greater. To help meet this growing demand, the Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI) has opened applications for two prestigious fellowship programs: the GovAI UK Winter Fellowship 2027 (Research Track) and the GovAI DC Winter Fellowship 2027. These highly competitive three-month fellowships are designed to help aspiring and experienced researchers, policy professionals, academics, and emerging technology specialists launch or accelerate impactful careers in AI governance and public policy. Both fellowships offer participants the opportunity to conduct independent research, receive mentorship from leading experts, engage with influential policy networks, and develop expertise in one of the world s fastest-growing policy fields. Successful fellows will join GovAI in either London, United Kingdom, or Washington, DC, United States, where they will work full-time on research projects that contribute to understanding and improving governance for advanced artificial intelligence systems. Applications for both programs are currently open, with a deadline of 12 July 2026. About GovAI The Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI) is one of the world s leading organizations dedicated to understanding and improving governance approaches for advanced artificial intelligence. Founded at Yale University in 2016, GovAI was among the first academic organizations to systematically study the policy implications of AI progress. Since publishing its influential research agenda in 2018, GovAI has helped define the emerging field of AI governance and continues to provide research and policy guidance to governments, companies, and civil society organizations worldwide. Today, GovAI operates as an independent nonprofit think tank focused on helping decision-makers navigate the transition toward a future shaped by increasingly capable AI systems. Its researchers and affiliates possess expertise in: AI governance Frontier AI regulation AI economics Risk analysis Threat modeling AI forecasting Technical governance Agent governance International cooperation US-China relations Cybersecurity Biosecurity AI safety GovAI researchers have published work in internationally recognized venues including: Science Nature NeurIPS FAccT Why GovAI Fellowships Are Highly Respected One reason these fellowships are particularly attractive is the strength of GovAI s professional network. GovAI alumni have secured influential roles in: Governments White House Office of Science and Technology Policy National Security Council U.S. Senate Department of Homeland Security Department of Commerce UK Government European Union institutions Leading AI Companies OpenAI Anthropic Google DeepMind Think Tanks RAND Center for Security and Emerging Technology (CSET) Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS) Center for a New American Security (CNAS) Foundation for American Innovation Universities Yale University Stanford University Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) University of Oxford University of Cambridge For individuals seeking long-term careers in AI governance, technology policy, national security, regulation, or public policy, these fellowships provide exceptional exposure and networking opportunities. Understanding the Two Fellowship Tracks Although both fellowships share similar goals, they focus on different policy ecosystems and geographic contexts. GovAI UK Winter Fellowship 2027 (Research Track) The UK Fellowship is designed primarily for individuals interested in global AI governance and policy research. Participants conduct independent research while building expertise across international AI governance challenges. Fellowship Duration 18 January 2027 – 9 April 2027 Location London, United Kingdom Financial Support Selected fellows receive: £12,000 salary Travel support to London Temporary work visa sponsorship Office workspace Weekday lunches Focus Areas The UK fellowship supports research related to: Global AI governance AI regulation AI forecasting Risk management International cooperation Agent governance Technical governance Frontier AI policy AI economics Emerging technology governance Learn More Here GovAI DC Winter Fellowship 2027 The Washington, DC fellowship focuses specifically on AI policymaking within the United States political system. The program emphasizes: Bipartisan engagement Policy relevance Practical governance solutions Understanding how AI policy is shaped in the United States Fellowship Duration 18 January 2027 – 9 April 2027 Location Washington, DC, United States Financial Support Selected fellows receive: $21,000 stipend Travel support to Washington, DC Office workspace Weekday lunches Important Eligibility Note Applicants must already possess the legal right to work in the United States. GovAI does not sponsor visas for the DC Fellowship. Focus Areas The fellowship supports research and policy analysis connected to: U.S. AI policy Emerging technology governance National security and AI AI regulation Technology competition International technology policy Economic impacts of AI AI innovation and governance Learn More Here What Fellows Will Do Participants in both fellowships engage in a structured yet flexible research experience. Independent Research Projects Each fellow conducts a research project of their choice. Potential outputs include: Research reports White papers Academic papers Journal articles Policy briefs Op-eds Public-facing analyses Blog posts Dedicated Mentorship Each fellow is paired with a supervisor from GovAI s team or network. Supervisors provide: Research guidance Project feedback Career mentorship Publication advice Strategic direction AI Governance Exploration Phase During the first two weeks, fellows explore: Current policy debates Governance challenges Research gaps Emerging issues Priority policy questions They then refine their research proposal before beginning full project work. Professional Development Opportunities Both fellowships offer extensive training and professional development. Participants gain access to: Expert Q A Sessions Engage with leading voices in: AI governance Public policy National security Technology regulation Economics International relations Weekly Seminars Seminars deepen understanding of: AI policy frameworks Governance challenges Risk management approaches Regulatory developments Skills Workshops Workshops help fellows strengthen: Policy writing Research methodology Communication skills Strategic analysis Stakeholder engagement Peer Feedback Sessions Work-in-progress meetings allow fellows to: Share ideas Receive feedback Improve research quality Collaborate with peers Networking Events Participants build relationships with: Policymakers Researchers Academics Technology leaders Think tank experts Who Should Apply? GovAI encourages applications from a broad range of professional and academic backgrounds. Ideal candidates may include: Researchers and Academics PhD students Postdoctoral researchers Faculty members Independent scholars Policy Professionals Government officials Policy advisors Legislative staff Public sector analysts Technology Professionals Engineers AI researchers Product specialists Technology strategists Civil Society Leaders Advocacy professionals NGO practitioners Public-interest researchers Students Both programs welcome strong candidates at earlier career stages, including undergraduates and graduate students. Relevant Academic and Professional Backgrounds Applicants may come from fields including: Public Policy Political Science Economics Computer Science Engineering Biosecurity Cybersecurity International Relations China Studies Risk Management Law Governance Technology Policy Importantly, there are no strict degree requirements. Selection Criteria Applicants are evaluated based on: Quality of Work Demonstrated ability to produce: High-quality analysis Well-reasoned arguments Clear writing Insightful research Relevant Expertise Knowledge or experience that may contribute to AI governance research. Judgment Ability to: Prioritize effectively Evaluate opportunities Assess policy relevance Make sound decisions Team Fit Candidates should demonstrate: Intellectual honesty Curiosity Collaboration Openness to feedback Professional integrity Application Process The application process is identical for both fellowships. Stage 1: Written Application Applicants submit: Written application materials Background information Experience details Selected candidates complete a 20-minute abstract reasoning assessment. Stage 2: Paid Remote Work Test Invitations issued during August 2026. Stage 3: Interview and Reference Checks Conducted remotely during September 2026. Final Decisions Expected in October 2026. Click HERE to apply for the GovAI UK Winter Fellowship 2027, Research Track. Click HERE to apply for the GovAI DC Winter Fellowship 2027. Important Deadline Application Deadline: 12 July 2026 UK Fellowship: 23:59 BST DC Fellowship: 11:59 PM ET Why These Fellowships Matter As artificial intelligence continues to reshape global systems, governments and institutions urgently need professionals capable of understanding and governing advanced AI technologies. The GovAI Winter Fellowships provide participants with: Financial support Mentorship Professional development Policy exposure Global networking Research opportunities Career acceleration Few opportunities combine these elements at such a high level. Conclusion The GovAI UK Winter Fellowship 2027 and GovAI DC Winter Fellowship 2027 represent two of the most prestigious opportunities available for individuals interested in artificial intelligence governance, technology policy, public policy, and emerging technology research. Whether applicants are interested in global AI governance from London or U.S.-focused AI policy from Washington, DC, both fellowships offer unparalleled access to expert mentorship, policy networks, independent research opportunities, and meaningful career development. With generous financial support, world-class training, and direct engagement with leaders shaping the future of AI governance, these programs provide an exceptional pathway for researchers, students, policy professionals, and technology experts seeking to contribute to one of the most important policy challenges of our time. Interested candidates should submit their applications before the 12 July 2026 deadline. Discover more global Fellowship , Job and AI opportunities on Opportunities for Youth (OFY). title: British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026–27 at the University of Oxford: Fully Funded Three-Year Research Opportunity for Early Career Scholars
summary: The Faculty of Asian and Middle Eastern Studies (AMES) at the University of Oxford has officially opened applications for expressions of interest for the prestigious British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026–27. This highly competitive fellowship provides an exceptional opportunity for outstanding early-career researchers in the Humanities and Social Sciences to advance their academic careers within one of the world s leading universities. The British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowship scheme is designed to support promising scholars who have recently completed their doctoral studies or are nearing completion. Through this three-year fellowship, successful candidates will gain valuable experience in both research and teaching while developing a strong academic profile that can significantly enhance their prospects for securing permanent academic positions in the future. About the British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowship The British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowship is one of the United Kingdom s most respected funding schemes for early-career researchers. It aims to provide outstanding scholars with the time, resources, and institutional support necessary to pursue innovative research projects while gaining valuable university-level teaching experience. Hosted through the Faculty of Asian and Middle Eastern Studies at the University of Oxford, the fellowship offers scholars the opportunity to work within a world-renowned academic environment known for excellence in research, teaching, and interdisciplinary collaboration. The fellowship specifically supports scholars working within: Humanities disciplines Social Science disciplines Interdisciplinary research projects that align with the British Academy s objectives Research related to Asian, Middle Eastern, and associated studies where appropriate Why Apply for the British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowship? The fellowship offers a unique platform for early-career researchers to establish themselves within academia and build a strong foundation for long-term success. Key benefits include: 1. Three Years of Dedicated Research Support Successful fellows receive the opportunity to focus extensively on their research agenda over a three-year period, allowing them to develop substantial scholarly outputs and advance their academic expertise. 2. Teaching Experience in a World-Class Institution The fellowship enables scholars to gain practical teaching experience within a university environment, helping them develop essential academic skills that are highly valued for future faculty positions. 3. Enhanced Career Development The scheme is specifically designed to strengthen participants research and teaching portfolios, improving their competitiveness for permanent academic appointments at leading universities worldwide. 4. Access to Oxford s Academic Community Fellows become part of one of the world s most prestigious academic communities, benefiting from: International research networks Academic seminars and conferences Interdisciplinary collaborations Access to exceptional libraries and research resources Mentorship from leading scholars in their field 5. Professional Mentorship Applicants are required to identify a mentor within the Faculty of Asian and Middle Eastern Studies. This mentorship relationship provides valuable academic guidance, professional support, and opportunities for scholarly development throughout the fellowship process. Expression of Interest Process at Oxford The Faculty of Asian and Middle Eastern Studies has established an internal selection process for candidates wishing to apply through Oxford. Interested scholars must first submit an expression of interest to the Faculty before proceeding to the British Academy application stage. Required Application Materials Applicants should prepare and submit: A completed application form. Details of their proposed research project. Supporting information demonstrating academic excellence and suitability for the fellowship. Any additional materials requested by the Faculty. Mentor Requirement An important component of the application process is securing a prospective mentor within the Faculty. Applicants must: Identify a suitable mentor within the Faculty of Asian and Middle Eastern Studies. Obtain the mentor s agreement to support the application. Ensure the mentor submits an email confirming their willingness to serve in this role. Have the mentor provide a brief assessment of the proposed application. It is important to note that while applicants must secure mentor agreement before the deadline, mentors are not expected to contribute directly to the preparation of applications at this initial stage. Additional Circumstances Statement The Faculty recognizes that personal or professional circumstances may affect an individual s research trajectory. Applicants may submit a separate statement outlining circumstances that may have impacted their research record, including but not limited to: Illness Parental leave Caring responsibilities Other significant personal circumstances Providing such information allows the Faculty to consider research achievements within the appropriate context. Selection and Next Steps Applications received by the Faculty will undergo internal review. Candidates selected for institutional support will be contacted by the University s Research Facilitation team and provided with further information regarding: Application procedures Submission requirements Fellowship timelines British Academy application system access Additional documentation requirements Only candidates selected through Oxford s internal process will proceed to the next stage of the fellowship competition. Important Deadline Expression of Interest Submission Deadline Date: Wednesday, 1 July 2026 Time: 17:00 (5:00 PM) Applicants must ensure that: Their application form is submitted by the deadline. Their mentor s supporting email is submitted by the same deadline. All required materials are received on time. Late applications may not be supported or processed by the Faculty. How to Apply Candidates interested in applying through the Faculty of Asian and Middle Eastern Studies at the University of Oxford should submit their application form via email to: amy.richardson@humanities.ox.ac.uk Applicants should also arrange for their prospective mentor to submit the required confirmation and assessment to the same email address before the deadline. Final Thoughts The British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026–27 represent an outstanding opportunity for emerging scholars to accelerate their academic careers through a prestigious three-year fellowship at the University of Oxford. By combining advanced research opportunities, teaching experience, professional mentorship, and access to a globally recognized academic environment, the fellowship provides an ideal pathway for talented researchers seeking to establish themselves as future leaders in the Humanities and Social Sciences. Eligible candidates are encouraged to begin discussions with potential mentors as early as possible and prepare their application materials well ahead of the deadline to maximize their chances of success. For more information, visit the official website. title: ASPIRE Fellows Program 2026: Work with Governments, Scale Evidence-Based Solutions, and Transform Public Policy in India
summary: Young professionals seeking a meaningful career in development, public policy, governance, and evidence-based social impact now have an exceptional opportunity to work directly with government systems through the ASPIRE Fellows Program hosted by J-PAL South Asia. The ASPIRE Fellowship is a prestigious two-year professional engagement designed to bridge the gap between research and policy implementation. Through this unique initiative, fellows work alongside researchers, government departments, policymakers, and civil society organizations to integrate evidence-based solutions into large-scale government programs across India. Hosted by J-PAL South Asia, the fellowship provides emerging development professionals with unparalleled exposure to policy implementation, government partnerships, program scaling, monitoring and evaluation, and public sector innovation. For individuals passionate about translating research into real-world impact, the ASPIRE Fellows Program offers a unique platform to contribute to transformative change while building a strong foundation for a long-term career in the development sector. What Is the ASPIRE Fellows Program? The ASPIRE Fellows Program is an immersive professional fellowship that enables young professionals with two to five years of experience to work closely with J-PAL South Asia s extensive network of: Researchers Government ministries State government departments Development partners Civil society organizations Technical experts Implementation agencies The fellowship focuses on scaling evidence-based interventions through government systems. Rather than working solely in research environments, fellows become embedded within government departments and institutions, helping policymakers implement programs backed by rigorous evidence and evaluation. The program creates a critical connection between research findings and practical implementation, ensuring that effective interventions can be expanded to reach millions of beneficiaries. About J-PAL South Asia J-PAL South Asia is part of the global Abdul Latif Jameel Poverty Action Lab (J-PAL), a leading research center dedicated to reducing poverty through evidence-informed policy. Through partnerships with governments, researchers, and development organizations, J-PAL promotes the use of rigorous evidence to design, test, and scale programs that improve lives. The ASPIRE Fellowship represents one of J-PAL South Asia s key initiatives to strengthen the use of evidence within public systems and government decision-making processes. Fellowship Duration and Structure The ASPIRE Fellowship is a full-time, two-year engagement. Phase 1: Intensive Onboarding at J-PAL South Asia During the first two months, selected fellows are placed at J-PAL South Asia s Delhi office. This onboarding phase includes: Intensive orientation Technical training Exposure to evidence-based policymaking Understanding government partnerships Learning about implementation science Familiarization with program scaling methodologies Professional development and mentorship This foundation prepares fellows for successful deployment within government systems. Phase 2: Government Placement After completing onboarding, fellows are placed within designated government departments and offices according to their assigned projects. These placements allow fellows to work directly within government structures and contribute to the implementation and scaling of high-impact interventions. Goals of the ASPIRE Fellows Program The fellowship is built around two major objectives. 1. Strengthening Collaboration Between Research and Government The program seeks to foster convergence among: J-PAL South Asia Policymakers Government systems Technical partners Development practitioners By strengthening these partnerships, the fellowship supports the effective scaling of interventions proven to work through rigorous evidence. 2. Developing Knowledge on Scaling Evidence-Based Programs The fellowship contributes to building locally grounded knowledge by: Documenting lessons learned Capturing implementation insights Studying challenges in scaling programs Identifying best practices Supporting continuous learning This knowledge helps improve future development interventions across India. What Kind of Work Do ASPIRE Fellows Do? ASPIRE Fellows serve as critical links between research, implementation, and policymaking. Their responsibilities may include: Program Management Coordinating large-scale initiatives Supporting implementation activities Managing project timelines Tracking program performance Stakeholder Engagement Working with government officials Facilitating collaboration among partners Supporting communication across institutions Building relationships with key stakeholders Technical Assistance Supporting policy implementation Providing evidence-based recommendations Assisting program design and refinement Contributing to strategic planning Monitoring and Evaluation Supporting data collection efforts Coordinating evaluations Interpreting research findings Translating evidence into actionable recommendations Organizational Learning Documenting lessons learned Sharing best practices Facilitating knowledge management Strengthening institutional learning systems Meet the Impact: Examples of Current ASPIRE Fellows The experiences of current fellows demonstrate the breadth and significance of projects supported through the program. Sanjana Chettri Sanjana serves as an ASPIRE Fellow supporting the Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM), a flagship initiative under India s Ministry of Rural Development. Her work focuses on: Coordinating learning and knowledge-building activities Supporting the establishment of a Gender Impact Lab Designing evidence-based solutions for rural women Strengthening learning systems within DAY-NRLM Prior to joining the fellowship, she worked with organizations such as: Oxfam Belongg 60 Decibels She also led a preventive mental health initiative in partnership with the Government of Delhi. Nidhi Arun Nidhi supports innovative education programs implemented through Karnataka s Department of School Education and Literacy. Her work involves: Supporting program design Scaling educational interventions Coordinating impact evaluations Building policy evidence Programs she supports include: ConnectED Maru Sinchana Every Child Counts Gender Equity Curriculum Before joining the fellowship, she worked with: Indiana University Public Policy Institute Government Economic Service (United Kingdom) International Finance Corporation (IFC) These examples illustrate the diversity of opportunities available to fellows across sectors including education, gender, livelihoods, and governance. Who Should Apply? The fellowship is designed for highly motivated professionals who want to contribute to meaningful public sector transformation. Ideal applicants are individuals who: Want to build a career in development. Have a strong interest in public policy. Enjoy solving complex implementation challenges. Are passionate about social impact. Believe in evidence-based decision-making. Want to work directly with government systems. Seek hands-on experience in scaling interventions. Skills and Competencies Sought Successful applicants are expected to demonstrate strong capabilities in the following areas. Stakeholder Management and Facilitation Candidates should be able to: Build partnerships Coordinate diverse stakeholders Facilitate discussions Manage relationships effectively Program Management Applicants should possess: Project planning skills Organizational abilities Time management capabilities Coordination experience Technical Assistance Strong analytical and problem-solving skills are valuable for supporting government programs and policy implementation. Organizational Learning Applicants should be interested in generating, documenting, and sharing knowledge to improve future interventions. Understanding of Monitoring and Evaluation Knowledge of monitoring and evaluation is considered highly valuable because fellows frequently engage with: Evidence generation Impact assessments Learning systems Program improvement processes Candidates with M E experience may be particularly competitive. Benefits of Becoming an ASPIRE Fellow The fellowship offers numerous professional advantages. Professional Development Fellows receive: Intensive onboarding Practical training Mentorship opportunities Exposure to leading researchers Government Experience Participants gain first-hand experience working within government systems and public institutions. Large-Scale Impact Fellows contribute to programs that affect millions of people across India. Evidence-Based Policy Exposure Participants learn how rigorous research informs policymaking and implementation. High-Level Networking Fellows build relationships with: Government leaders Researchers Development experts International organizations Civil society actors Career Advancement The fellowship provides exceptional experience for future careers in: Public policy International development Government consulting Monitoring and evaluation Research and implementation Program management Application Process Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis. Step 1: Submit an Application Candidates must submit: Curriculum Vitae (CV) Cover Letter Short Video Introduction The video should introduce: The applicant Their background Their motivation for joining the fellowship Their interest in evidence-based development Step 2: Case Study Assessment Shortlisted candidates will be invited to complete a case study or designated assignment. This stage evaluates: Analytical thinking Problem-solving abilities Communication skills Understanding of development challenges Step 3: Interview Rounds Successful candidates proceed to two rounds of interviews with the J-PAL South Asia team. The interviews assess: Technical competencies Professional experience Motivation Alignment with fellowship objectives Projects and Government Partners Fellows may be assigned to projects involving: Ministry of Education Supporting educational innovation, learning outcomes, and policy implementation. Ministry of Rural Development Working on large-scale livelihood, gender, and rural development programs. Additional opportunities may emerge through J-PAL South Asia s expanding network of government and development partners. How to Apply Interested candidates should prepare: An updated CV. A compelling cover letter. A short introductory video. Applications are accepted through the ASPIRE Fellows Program platform. For questions regarding the fellowship or application process, applicants may contact: vasharma@povertyactionlab.org tpande@povertyactionlab.org Application Deadline No fixed deadline has been announced. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis. Interested candidates are strongly encouraged to apply as early as possible. Why the ASPIRE Fellowship Stands Out Few opportunities allow young professionals to work directly at the intersection of research, government, and large-scale implementation. The ASPIRE Fellows Program provides a rare chance to embed within public systems, influence policy implementation, strengthen evidence use in governance, and contribute to meaningful development outcomes. For ambitious professionals seeking to build expertise in development, public policy, monitoring and evaluation, and government partnerships, the ASPIRE Fellowship offers an outstanding pathway to accelerate both professional growth and social impact.
